messages.content:
Story set in Supergirl tv show. Story about Lena Luthor & Nicolas. romance & sensual undertone
Nicolas description for references: 1m78, and a bit overweight, short skipy brown hairs & brown eyes behind glasses. He doesn’t grow past his shyness & awkward way with Lena. He is just a delivery guy new in town. Taking cooking class with Alex Danvers. 
Lena Luthor is more assertive in this story & don’t second guess on her desires. Lena finds refreshing that someone doesn’t seem to care about her family name . She will be the one initiating each step in their growing relationship. For future references: 1: Nicolas calls her « Miss Lena » until further notice. No sexual relations without further notice. 
Lena Luthor is finding herself in a journey to slowly seduce Nicolas. He is a breath of fresh hair for her even if he only manages to say « hello » and « have a good day to her ». Nicolas never get past his shy & akward demeanor with Lena . It is something she find alluring. Lena find herself curious about this man that doesn’t recognize her when he has a delivery for her. Write Lena and Nicolas first meeting.

Lena Luthor was in the middle of a busy day at L Corp when she received a notification on her phone that her lunch had been delivered. She had ordered a salad with kale and quinoa, but when she arrived in the lobby to retrieve it, she was met with a shy man in glasses holding a large pizza box.

"Uh, miss, I think there's a mistake," he said, his voice soft and hesitant. "I was supposed to deliver a pizza to this address."

Lena raised an eyebrow, taking a closer look at the delivery label. It was indeed her address, but she had definitely not ordered a pizza.

"That's odd," she said, taking the box from him. "Well, thank you for the delivery. How much do I owe you?"

The man fumbled with his notepad, his eyes darting between her and his written notes.

"Uh, let me see… it should be… seven dollars and fifty cents," he finally said, his cheeks turning red.

Lena handed him a ten-dollar bill, giving him a small smile. "Keep the change," she said, before walking away.

She couldn't help but feel a little curious about the delivery man. He seemed so nervous and out of place in the bustling lobby of L Corp. For a moment, she considered trying to strike up a conversation with him, but she shook her head and dismissed the thought. She had important meetings to attend and couldn't afford to waste her time on a delivery guy.

But as the day went on, Lena found herself thinking about the man with the pizza box more than she cared to admit. Something about his shyness and unassuming nature intrigued her. She found herself wondering what he was like outside of his job and imagined what it would be like to get to know him better.

Little did she know, fate would soon bring them together in unexpected ways.
At a girls diner with Alex and Kara, Lena learn all about the walking disaster this guy, a man going by Nicolas, is in the kitchen.

Lena sat across from Alex and Kara at their favorite girls' diner as they chatted about their day. Lena had been lost in thought, her mind wandering back to the shy delivery guy who had delivered her lunch that day. She couldn't shake him out of her mind.

Alex noticed the faraway look in Lena's eyes and asked, "What's on your mind, Lena?"

Lena let out a sigh and shook her head. "It's nothing, just a guy who delivered my lunch today. He seemed so nervous and timid, I can't seem to get him out of my head."

Kara beamed at her sister. "Ooooh, a crush! Tell us more!"

Lena rolled her eyes but couldn't help but let out a small laugh. "It's not like that. His name is Nicolas, he works as a delivery guy. But what do you mean, tell you more?"

Alex took a sip of her coffee before explaining, "Oh, you know how we're always trying to find new ways to make cooking more interesting? Well, we both signed up for a cooking class to learn some new skills and Nicolas is in our class."

Lena's eyes widened. "Really? What's he like?"

Alex and Kara shared a knowing glance before launching into their stories of the walking disaster that was Nicolas in the kitchen. They told Lena about how he would always spill things and burn the food, and yet he somehow managed to charm the entire class with his shy smile and endearing personality.

Lena found herself smiling at their stories, feeling even more drawn to this man who seemed so genuine and kind. She knew that she wanted to get to know him better and learn more about who he was outside of his job.

After their meal, Lena thanked Alex and Kara for the company and made her way back to L Corp. As she walked through the lobby, she couldn't help but scan the area, hoping to catch a glimpse of Nicolas delivering another package. She knew that she would have to be patient and let fate take its course, but she couldn't help the growing sense of anticipation that buzzed in her chest.
Alex and Kara tease a bit Nicolas about his « skills » in destroying the kitchen. Then ask him if he as someone to impress with his cooking since he seem so enamored with the punishment.

The next day at their cooking class, Alex and Kara couldn't help but tease Nicolas about his cooking skills as they watched him struggle to chop vegetables without causing a minor disaster in the kitchen. Lena watched from a distance, her eyes trained on Nicolas as he fumbled with the knife.

"Nicolas, are you sure you're not secretly trying to destroy the kitchen on purpose?" Kara teased him, a playful glint in her eye.

Alex joined in, "Or do you have someone to impress with all these cooking mishaps?"

Nicolas's cheeks turned pink as he chuckled nervously. "I wish. Cooking is just something I've always been interested in, even though I'm not very good at it."

Lena saw his downcast expression and felt a sudden urge to defend him. "Don't worry about it, Nicolas. Nobody's perfect, and the important thing is that you're passionate about cooking."

Nicolas's face lit up at the sound of Lena's voice. "Thanks, Miss Lena," he said, his smile growing wider.

As they cooked, Lena couldn't help but take note of how Nicolas's demeanor around her seemed to shift slightly. He was still shy and awkward, but there was a subtle confidence in the way he talked to her, an eagerness to learn more about her and who she was beyond her job at L Corp.

As the class ended, Lena made her way over to Nicolas, who was packing up his things. "Hey, do you maybe want to grab a coffee with me sometime?" she asked, surprising herself with her boldness.

Nicolas's eyes widened in surprise. "Me? Sure, I would love that."

As they walked out of the kitchen together, Lena felt a flutter of excitement in her chest. She knew that she was taking a risk by pursuing Nicolas, but she couldn't help the growing attraction she felt towards him. It was a new and exciting feeling, and one that she was more than willing to explore.
This time, Lena found herself the victim of her friends teasing while they have diner. Meanwhile, Nicolas reads a bit in his apartment, trying to figure who is the murderer in the story.

Lena found herself blushing as Alex and Kara teased her relentlessly about her date with Nicolas. They were both practically bouncing in their seats, eager to hear every detail of the coffee date.

"So, did he make you anything besides coffee?" Kara asked, a mischievous gleam in her eye.

Alex elbowed her sister, "Kara, don't be so nosy. Lena, how did it go?"

Lena tried to hide her smile but failed, "It went really well. We had a great conversation, and I think we have a lot in common."

The girls were all smiles until Alex's phone beeped, interrupting the conversation. "Sorry, I have to take this," she said, before answering the call.

While Alex was distracted, Kara leaned in conspiratorially. "So, are you going to see him again?"

Lena smirked, "I think so. We talked about trying some recipes together, and I think it could be really fun."

Kara let out a happy sigh, "Wow, Lena, I'm so happy for you!"

As the evening wore on, Lena found herself lost in thought about Nicolas and all the possibilities their future could hold. Meanwhile, Nicolas was at home, curled up with a cozy mystery novel, trying to unravel the clues to the murder mystery at the center of the story.

He couldn't help but think about Lena, her vibrant personality and easy smile filling his mind. He realized that even though he didn't know much about her yet, he was drawn to her in a way that he couldn't quite explain. As he read, his thoughts kept circling back to Lena, imagining what it would be like to cook with her, to explore the world around them together.

Despite the mystery novel in his hands, he couldn't shake the feeling that the biggest mystery in his life right now was the enigma that was Lena Luthor. And he couldn't wait to solve it with her by his side.
« I’ve à date with this beautiful woman. Alex please , i beg you, help me ! »

Nicolas's heart was pounding in his chest as he dialed Alex's number for the fifth time. He had been hoping to reach her for the past half-hour but she wasn't answering her phone. He couldn't believe that he was actually going on a date with Lena Luthor, the woman who he had admired from afar for so long.

Finally, Alex picked up the phone. "Nicolas? Is everything okay?"

"Alex, thank god you finally answered. Listen, I've got a date with this beautiful woman, and I have no idea what to wear or where to take her. Please, I beg you, help me!" Nicolas cried out in frustration.

Alex chuckled at his desperation. "Alright, alright, calm down. First things first, what are your plans for the date?"

Nicolas paused, trying to remember the details of the conversation he had had with Lena. "We talked about trying some recipes together, maybe something Italian?"

Alex smiled at the idea. "Okay, then why don't you wear something comfortable but nice, and take her to a little Italian trattoria I know, it's not too fancy but it's great for a date."

Nicolas felt a wave of relief wash over him. "Thank you, Alex, you're a lifesaver."

"No problem, buddy. Just remember to be yourself and have fun. And don't forget to breathe," Alex said, before hanging up the phone.

Nicolas took a deep breath and smiled to himself. He was about to go on a date with Lena Luthor, and maybe, just maybe, this could be the beginning of something special.
That’s it. He is in a date with Lena Luthor. Him. Shy and awkward Nicolas is having a date with Lena Luthor.

Nicolas could barely contain his excitement as he waited outside the trattoria for Lena to arrive. He tried to keep calm, his heart racing as he thought about how far he had come. From the shy and awkward delivery guy who had delivered lunch to the powerful CEO of L Corp to being on a date with Lena Luthor herself, it was hard to believe that this was really happening.

He fidgeted with his shirt collar and took a deep breath, trying to steady his nerves. Suddenly, he saw Lena walking towards him, her eyes lighting up as she saw him waiting for her.

"Hi, Nicolas," she said, giving him a warm smile. "You look great."

Nicolas felt his cheeks turning pink. "You look beautiful," he managed to say, before ushering her inside the trattoria.

As they made their way to their table, Nicolas couldn't help but feel a little nervous. He kept reminding himself to breathe, to stay calm, and to enjoy the moment.

Lena seemed to sense his anxiety and reached out to touch his hand. "Relax, Nicolas. This is just us, two people having dinner together."

Nicolas felt his nerves dissipating as he gazed into Lena's warm brown eyes. He couldn't believe that he, the shy and awkward delivery guy, was having a meaningful conversation with Lena Luthor, one of the most powerful women in National City.

As they ate their dinner, Nicolas found himself talking more and more, his confidence growing as he shared stories and jokes with Lena. She laughed at his jokes and listened intently to his anecdotes, and Nicolas felt like he had finally found someone who understood him.

As they finished their meal and prepared to leave, Nicolas felt a sense of sadness wash over him. He didn't want the night to end, not when he was just starting to feel comfortable in Lena's presence.

"Thank you for a great evening, Lena," he said, his voice soft and a little hesitant.

Lena smiled and leaned in to kiss him gently on the cheek. "The pleasure was all mine, Nicolas. I look forward to doing this again sometime."

Nicolas felt a surge of happiness and excitement in his chest. Maybe this was just the beginning of something amazing. The shy and awkward delivery guy had gone on a date with Lena Luthor, and now, anything seemed possible.
Kara, Alex and Lena gossiping ? Yes. About Lena date ? Of course. Is Nicolas a good kisser ? Lena can’t answer that yet.

Kara, Alex, and Lena were sitting in the girls' diner having their usual catch-up. Lena couldn't help but feel a little giddy, thinking about her date with Nicolas the night before. She tried to play it cool, but Alex and Kara seemed to sense her excitement.

"So, Lena, we have to know, how was your date with Nicolas?" Alex asked, a mischievous grin on her face.

Lena tried to hide her smile, "It was great. We had dinner at an Italian restaurant and talked for hours. He's really sweet and charming."

Kara leaned in, her eyes wide, "So, was he a good kisser?"

Lena's face turned red, "I don't know yet. Our first date ended with just a kiss on the cheek."

Alex and Kara groaned, "Come on, Lena, give us something to work with here."

Lena laughed, "Sorry to disappoint, but I'm not going to kiss and tell. Besides, I'm interested in getting to know Nicolas beyond just physical attraction."

The girls nodded in understanding, but couldn't help but tease Lena a little more. As they finished their meal and parted ways, Lena couldn't shake the feeling of excitement and anticipation that she felt. She knew that there was something special about Nicolas, something that drew her to him in a way that she couldn't explain.

As she walked back to L Corp, she couldn't help but think about their next date and what the future might hold for them. All she knew was that she was ready to take a chance on love, even if it meant stepping out of her comfort zone and taking a risk with someone who was a little shy and awkward like Nicolas.
How to not ruin your one and only chance with Lena Luthor. A 101 guide by Alex Danvers for Nicolas.

Alex pulled Nicolas aside at their next cooking class, a serious expression on her face. "Listen, Nicolas, I know you really like Lena, and I just want to make sure that you don't ruin your chance with her."

Nicolas looked at her, his eyes wide with concern. "What do you mean? What could I possibly do to ruin my chance with Lena?"

Alex put a hand on his shoulder. "Look, Lena is amazing, and she doesn't give her heart to just anyone. She's smart, successful, and confident, and she deserves someone who can match her wit and charm. I'm not saying that you're not those things, but you need to make sure that you bring your A-game when you're with her."

Nicolas nodded, listening intently.

Alex continued, "So, first of all, you need to be confident. Lena likes a man who knows what he wants and goes after it. Don't be afraid to take charge and show her that you're interested in her. You need to make her feel special and wanted."

Nicolas took a deep breath, thinking over Alex's advice.

"Secondly," Alex said, "you need to be interesting. Lena is a busy woman with a lot going on in her life. You need to have hobbies and interests outside of work that you can share with her. Be well-read, be knowledgeable about current events, and be curious about the world around you."

Nicolas nodded, feeling like this was all great advice.

"Last but not least," Alex said, "you need to be yourself. Don't try to be someone you're not just to impress Lena. She's looking for someone who is genuine and authentic. Remember that she agreed to go on a date with you because she likes you for who you are."

Nicolas felt a rush of gratitude towards Alex. He knew that he had a lot to offer, but he also knew that Lena was special, and he didn't want to mess things up with her. He felt like he had a better idea of what to do now, and he was determined to make things work with Lena, no matter what.
Alex reporting to Lena. Lena would just like Nicolas to relax a bit.

After class had finished, Alex couldn't resist sharing her advice with Lena on how Nicolas could impress her on their upcoming date.

"Lena, we need to talk. Since you're my best friend, I owe it to you to tell you everything I know."

Perking up, Lena's eyes widened in curiosity.

Alex dove straight in, "I gave Nicolas a lecture on how to behave on a date with someone like you. Don't worry, he's still the same nervous guy deep down. But now he's nervous with a purpose!"

Lena couldn't help but chuckle at Alex's enthusiasm.

"He's taking on board everything you said. He's confident, interesting, and most importantly, he's being himself," Alex reported. "So, what do you think?"

Lena paused for a moment, taking it all in. To her, Nicolas's shyness was endearing, and while Alex's advice was practical, she just hoped Nicolas would relax and be himself too.

"He doesn't need to act like anyone else, Alex. I like Nicolas just the way he is, shy and all."

Alex nodded in agreement, "I know, I know. But you have to admit, there's something sexy about a guy who takes your breath away with his confidence and charm."

Lena chuckled, "You're not wrong about that. But honestly, Alex, I just want to get to know the real Nicolas. I'm sure he's amazing
